description: Catalog of narrative templates available in the pptx.dev library. Each entry is a lightweight summary; full beat definitions live in the per-template JSON files alongside this index. Entries are grouped by usage: foundational arcs first, then pitches, sales and GTM, strategy and consulting, storytelling, keynotes and inspiration, rhythm-of-business reviews, data-driven analyses, and workforce reviews.

templates.name: Problem → Solution
templates.summary: Classic business arc: name the pain, raise the stakes, present your fix, show how it works, prove it, ask.

templates.name: SCQA (Situation–Complication–Question–Answer)
templates.summary: Minto's pyramid principle: surface the recommendation up front, then layer key arguments and supporting evidence beneath it.

templates.name: Strategic Narrative
templates.summary: Change-driven sales/founder arc: a tectonic shift creates winners and losers, and your product is the way to land in the winners' camp.

templates.name: Golden Circle (Why / How / What)
templates.summary: Sinek's keynote arc: lead with belief, follow with method, finish with proof.

templates.name: Conference Talk
templates.summary: Stage-talk arc for industry events: hook, promise, evidence, mechanism, application, recap.

templates.name: Transformation Arc (Before / After / Bridge)
templates.summary: Marketing-copy arc adapted for slides: paint the painful before, contrast the vivid after, deliver the bridge between them.

templates.name: Startup Pitch Deck
templates.summary: Canonical investor-pitch arc drawn from Sequoia and YC templates; optimized for 10–20 minute meetings.

templates.name: Early-Stage Startup Pitch
templates.summary: Seed and pre-seed pitch arc tuned for vision-heavy stories where revenue and traction are still thin.

templates.name: Persuasive Sales
templates.summary: Top-of-funnel sales arc anchored in market change, leading to your unique capabilities, supported by proof, ending with a clear ask.

templates.name: Company Intro
templates.summary: Standard company-overview deck: who you are, what's distinctive, who you serve, why they win.

templates.name: Marketing Strategy
templates.summary: Comprehensive go-to-market strategy arc structured around the 4Ps; for category launches and annual plans.

templates.name: Product Launch
templates.summary: Persuasive launch arc: brand, promise, conflict, stakes, reveal, raised stakes, sell, call to action.

templates.name: Strategic Advisory
templates.summary: Consulting-style advisory deck applying Minto's pyramid: title, SCQ, answer, three argued points, summary.

templates.name: Challenge → Resolution
templates.summary: Identify a current business challenge, trace its origins, and recommend an actionable resolution.

templates.name: Project Proposal
templates.summary: Internal proposal arc for greenlighting a project: hook, problem, idea, benefits, justification, plan, timeline, risks, outlook, ask.

templates.name: Failure Analysis
templates.summary: Walk through what went wrong, why, and what changes — a structured post-mortem for stakeholder reviews.

templates.name: Classic Story
templates.summary: Movie-style narrative arc applied to any topic: open the scene, introduce the villain, raise the stakes, send in the hero, deliver the plan, close with hope.

templates.name: Business Narrative
templates.summary: Tell a business strategy or solution as a story: setting, challenge, tension, hero, plan, outcome.

templates.name: Rags to Riches
templates.summary: Underdog journey from initial failure to eventual success — for founder stories and brand stories where the lesson is grit.

templates.name: Underdog Victory
templates.summary: Story arc of someone or something overlooked who triumphs in the end — for founder narratives and team-building decks.

templates.name: Survival Story
templates.summary: Narrative arc of overcoming adversity through determination — for brand stories and leadership talks where the lesson lives in the comeback.

templates.name: Educate
templates.summary: Engaging-explainer arc inspired by Mary Roach: surprise, evidence, anecdote, scrutiny, memorable insight.

templates.name: Persuade
templates.summary: Argument-driven keynote arc inspired by Ken Robinson: grip, challenge, story, statistic, revelation, proposal, question.

templates.name: Reveal
templates.summary: Insight-driven keynote arc inspired by Amy Cuddy: connect through story, ground in research, advise, demonstrate authenticity.

templates.name: Justice
templates.summary: Advocacy arc inspired by 'I Have a Dream': inequality, dream, urgency, unity, call to action, hope.

templates.name: Innovation
templates.summary: Keynote-style product reveal inspired by Steve Jobs's 2007 iPhone launch: stage, reveal, demonstrate, wow, differentiate, recategorize.

templates.name: Focus
templates.summary: Long-term, principle-driven business arc inspired by Bezos's 1997 shareholder letter: focus, performance, framework, discipline.

templates.name: Quarterly Business Review
templates.summary: Standard internal QBR arc: recap, performance, wins, challenges, insights, plan, risks, asks.

templates.name: Business Review
templates.summary: Full-loop review of recent activities — closer to a customer-facing or partner-facing relationship review than a status update.

templates.name: Board Meeting
templates.summary: Comprehensive board update covering CEO commentary, key metrics, financials, functional updates, and forward-looking topics.

templates.name: Weekly Progress
templates.summary: Tight rhythm-of-business arc for weekly progress reports — designed for fifteen-to-thirty-minute team check-ins.

templates.name: Status Update
templates.summary: Concise progress-report arc for keeping a stakeholder informed: headline, brief, progress, results, plan, feedback.

templates.name: Performance Review
templates.summary: Analytical review of individual, team, or company performance grounded in metrics and adjustments for the next period.

templates.name: Survey Analysis
templates.summary: Structured walk-through of survey results: topic, executive summary, methodology, findings, analysis, implications, recommendations.

templates.name: Trend Analysis
templates.summary: Identify and discuss data-driven patterns to understand performance and forecast what's next.

templates.name: Employee Review
templates.summary: Constructive performance-review arc that recognizes work, gives candid feedback, and sets growth goals together.

templates.name: Performance Improvement Plan
templates.summary: Structured PIP arc that helps a struggling employee see expectations, understand the gap, and walk a credible improvement path.

templates.name: Capacity Planning
templates.summary: Workforce and operational capacity-planning arc: kickoff, urgency, current state, projection, tactics, implementation, revision.
